title: The Splitter
content:
혻혻혻혻In the morn when the keen blade bites the tree,
혻혻혻혻And the chips on the dead leaves dance,
혻혻혻혻And the bush echoes back right merrily
혻혻혻혻Blow for blow as the sunbeams glance
혻혻혻혻From the axe when it sweeps in circles true,
혻혻혻혻Then the splitter at heart is gay;
혻혻혻혻He exults in the work he占퐏 set to do,
혻혻혻혻And he feels like a boy at play.

혻혻혻혻Swinging free with a stroke that占퐏 straight and strong
혻혻혻혻To the heart of the messmate sent,
혻혻혻혻He is cheered by the magpie占퐏 morning song
혻혻혻혻With the ring of the metal blent,
혻혻혻혻But the birds in their terror scatter high
혻혻혻혻When she falls with a rush and bound,
혻혻혻혻And the quivering saplings split and fly,
혻혻혻혻And the ranges all roar around.

혻혻혻혻Who is lord when the axeman mounts his spar,
혻혻혻혻And the breeze on his brown breast blows,
혻혻혻혻When the scent of the new wood floats afar,
혻혻혻혻And the gum from its red wounds flows?
혻혻혻혻With the bush at his back he laughs at care,
혻혻혻혻With a pipe and a right good mate,
혻혻혻혻There is drink in the billy, grub to spare,
혻혻혻혻And a bunk in the ten-by-eight.

혻혻혻혻When the sun占퐏 in the west, from nooks aloft
혻혻혻혻Where the stringy is straight and tall,
혻혻혻혻Come the strains of a chorus quaint and soft,
혻혻혻혻Or the clink of the wedge and maul;
혻혻혻혻From the gully a murmur of broken talk
혻혻혻혻Or the song that the crosscut sings;
혻혻혻혻For the bush is a-dream, and high the hawk
혻혻혻혻Hangs at rest on his cradling wings.

혻혻혻혻But at night, by the tent, when tea is done
혻혻혻혻And when euchre占퐏 begun to flag,
혻혻혻혻In the bush he may hear a distant gun
혻혻혻혻Or the neigh of a lonely nag,
혻혻혻혻Then the splitter has thoughts no longer gay,
혻혻혻혻And sorrows he cannot drown,
혻혻혻혻For he dreams of a girl who占퐏 far away,
혻혻혻혻Or the joys of a spree in town.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Dyson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Edward Dyson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
